body: import java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException;
import junit.framework.TestCase;

translation: self.assertEqual("Single Null", "Regular expression[0] is missing", e.getMessage())
self.assertEqual("Single Zero Length", "Regular expression[0] is missing", e.getMessage())
self.assertEqual("Null Array", "Regular expressions are missing", e.getMessage())
self.assertEqual("Zero Length Array", "Regular expressions are missing", e.getMessage())
self.assertEqual("Array has Null", "Regular expression[1] is missing", e.getMessage())
self.fail("Array has Zero Length - expected IllegalArgumentException")
self.assertEqual("Array has Zero Length", "Regular expression[0] is missing", e.getMessage())

translation: def __checkArray(self, label: str, expect: typing.List[typing.List[str]], result: typing.List[typing.List[str]]) -> None:
if expect is None or result is None:
if expect is None and result is None:
self.fail(label + " Null expect=" + str(expect) + " result=" + str(result))
return  # not strictly necessary, but prevents possible NPE below
self.fail(label + " Length expect=" + str(len(expect)) + " result=" + str(len(result)))

body: private static final String[] MULTIPLE_REGEX = new String[] {REGEX_1, REGEX_2, REGEX_3};
private static final String REGEX_3 = "^" + COMPONENT_1 + COMPONENT_2 + COMPONENT_3 + "$";

private static final String REGEX_2 =
            "^" + COMPONENT_1 + SEPARATOR_2 + COMPONENT_2 + SEPARATOR_2 + COMPONENT_3 + "$";

private static final String REGEX_1 =
            "^" + COMPONENT_1 + SEPARATOR_1 + COMPONENT_2 + SEPARATOR_1 + COMPONENT_3 + "$";
private static final String SEPARATOR_2 = "(?:\\s)";
private static final String SEPARATOR_1 = "(?:\\-)";
private static final String COMPONENT_3 = "([123]{3})";
private static final String COMPONENT_2 = "([DEF]{3})";
private static final String COMPONENT_1 = "([abc]{3})";
private static final String REGEX = "^([abc]*)(?:\\-)([DEF]*)(?:\\-)([123]*)$";
